binary_or_command: ANCHOR_TEST_EACH_SLEEP_SECONDS=15 bash scripts/anchor-test-each.sh (11 TS suites, isolated solana-test-validator per file)
new_findings: anchor-test-each adjudicator: aggregate anchor test 16 failures are pure validator cross-pollution (shared PDAs 12y3Uh6s..., 8SFqwq..., BqScmyjm...) when running 13 files in one ledger. Per-file isolation: ALL GREEN at 310 passing. Lombard protocol behavior is correctly verified; no protocol bug surfaced.

binary_or_command: direct read of EVM Mailbox.sol _deliverAndHandle and Solana mailbox handle_message.rs; comparison of failure semantics
new_findings: new_signal_id=SIG-XR-003-EVM-DIVERGENCE: EVM Mailbox._deliverAndHandle wraps IHandler.handlePayload in try/catch and only sets handledPayload[payloadHash]=true on success; handler revert does NOT bubble, message remains Delivered and re-attemptable. Solana mailbox.handle_message writes Handled then invoke_signed, Anchor tx atomicity rolls back Handled on recipient CPI revert. Both layers still gate literal replays via PDA init dedupe keyed on [MESSAGE_SEED, payload_hash]. Net effect: Solana requires a brand-new tx after failed gmp_receive; EVM allows retry through deliverAndHandle.

PROP-XR-EVM-006 confirmed: handler revert absorbed by Mailbox._deliverAndHandle try/catch; payload remains re-attemptable (MessageHandled re-runs handler each call); 2-test pair passes. PROP-XR-EVM-007 confirmed: AssetRouter.changeBascule(address(0)) under DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE disables Bascule.validateMint entirely; same payload, no report, mint proceeds and totalSupply grows by mint amount. Non-admin caller is rejected.

binary_or_command: ANCHOR_MOCHA_FILES=tests/ccip.ts anchor test --skip-build -- --features localnet --no-default-features (added v6.51 negative-path tests N1/N2/N3 inside 'incoming CCIP bridge operation' describe, reusing prep)
new_findings: PROP-XR-002 refined: N1 proven — mailbox.deliver does NOT gate destination_caller; only token_pool.release_or_mint does. Wrong destination_caller is stored verbatim; userTA balance untouched at mailbox layer. N2 proven — re-init at same nonce PDA reverts (ConstraintSeeds/init dedupe) confirms cross-replay safety. N3 proven — sourcePoolAddress that has no remoteChainConfig entry is accepted by offramp.init but reverts on executeOfframp, closing the cross-layer lookup chain.

binary_or_command: crucible run lombard_token_pool invariant_test -C crucible/lombard_token_pool_scaffold --dry-run; -j 1 --timeout 5 (stateful smoke)
new_findings: Crucible scaffold now loads mainnet-feature lombard_token_pool.so with 3 actions (type_version, derive_accounts_release_or_mint, derive_accounts_lock_or_burn). Dry-run PASS: - 2 tracked accounts - 1 programs loaded - Single iteration completed successfully - Harness validation passed. Stateful 5s smoke: 4 executions / 0 crashes / no invariant violation. Deeper CPIs (release_or_mint/lock_or_burn/mailbox) deferred to next session since they require loading bridge.so+mailbox.so+consortium.so+bascule_gmp.so into the same Crucible context with full account wiring.

Confirmed by direct source review + Rust probe: post_session_signatures indexes current_validators[index as usize] / current_weights[index as usize] / session.signed[index as usize] without bounds check. MAX_VALIDATOR_SET_SIZE=102; index >= len(current_validators) would panic in pure Rust and surface as tx failure on Solana. Reachable impact is session DoS only (no fund-loss CPI path before panic). Pure-Rust probe in programs/consortium/src/utils/post_session_signatures_probe.rs: 3 tests pass confirming the OOB pattern. Adjudication: honest-zero for fund-loss; informational DoS-only signal. Recommendation: add require!(*index < current_validators.len() as u64, ConsortiumError::ValidatorIndexOutOfBounds).

new_findings: Multi-program Crucible scaffold round: loaded 8 programs (lombard_token_pool + 7 sister) into a single TestContext at canonical localnet IDs from sources/lombard-finance/repo/target/deploy/*.so. Dry-run PASS: 2 tracked accounts, 8 programs loaded, harness validation passed. 8-second stateful smoke: 21076 iterations / 0 crashes / 5/5 actions discovered. Action set: action_type_version, action_derive_accounts_release_or_mint, action_derive_accounts_lock_or_burn, action_bootstrap_multi_program, action_config_pda_uniqueness_check. 68 unit tests pass across 19 crates: abi_utils 7, arrayvec 3, asset_router 4, bascule 1, bascule_gmp 2, base_token_pool 8, bridge 1, build_commit 1, ccip_common 7, consortium 7 (incl. DoS probe), lbtc 14, lombard_token_pool 2, mailbox 5, mailbox_receiver 1, mock_ccip_offramp 1, mock_ccip_rmn 1, ratio_oracle 1, registry 1, rmn_remote 1. Crucible-based adversarial release_or_mint_tokens CPI integration BLOCKED because TestContext::add_program does not expose bpf_loader_upgradeable upgrade-authority provisioning. lombard_token_pool::initialize && consortium::initialize both require upgrade-authority signer; cannot synthesise from fresh Keypair in current Crucible API. Strategy STRAT-S7-release-or-mint-rollback-crucible-blocked.md records the engine boundary. R3 carry-forward: validator/bankrun fixture, which natively provisions the upgrade-authority for localnet cluster.

binary_or_command: node_modules/.bin/ts-mocha -p ./tsconfig.json -t 60000 tests/ccip.ts --exit (yarn not on PATH; env classified block)
new_findings: Env-blocked. Test code for N4 (N4 v6.51: failed executeOfframp ROLLS BACK mailbox messageInfo.status) is now appended inside tests/ccip.ts 'incoming CCIP bridge operation' describe. Drives initOfframp(203, mintArg={amount:wrongAmountLE}), expects executeOfframp(203) to reject, then re-reads messageInfoPDA and asserts byte 8 == 1 (MessageState::Delivered) still. Solana test validator started via solana-test-validator --bpf-program keypair.so did NOT replicate anchor deploy's upgradeable-loader setup; consortium.initialize rejected with 'Attempt to load a program that does not exist'. Strategy STRAT-S8 documents the env-block. Replay requires session with `yarn run ts-mocha` available, which is provided by the night-shift cron (hermes/scripts/anchor-test-each.sh).

PROP-XR-EVM-010 added: Mailbox.deliverAndHandle does NOT maintain a per-handler processed set. Across 3 enabled re-deliveries MessageReceived fires 3 times; on interleaved disabled path MessageReceived fires 0 times; recover via enable() reflects next MessageReceived. Hardhat shows 6/6 passing in 6s. Confirms the contrast with Solana atomic rollback: EVM keeps the message re-attemptable indefinitely across the same deliveredPayload[payloadHash] scope; only the bridge-side payloadSpent single-shot dedupe protects against handler-level double-execution. Evidence at evidence/evm-prop-cross-layer-divergence-with-prop-010.log.

binary_or_command: crucible run lombard_token_pool invariant_test --dry-run; crucible run -j 1 lombard_token_pool invariant_test --timeout 8 --stateful
new_findings: R5 carry-forward action added: action_post_session_payload drives consortium::post_session_payload via raw_call with the first 8 bytes of sha256('global:post_session_payload') as the Anchor discriminator. Pre-computed POST_SESSION_PAYLOAD_DISC const avoids pulling solana_program::hash into the compile graph (incompatible with the workaround). Crucible stateful: 6/6 actions discovered (the new one is the first to write chain state on a sister program), 4781 iterations / 0 crashes / 49.0% ok-rate (drop from ~100% because init_if_needed PDAs succeed once per (payer, hash) pair). The harness now exercises a non-upgrade-gated sister instruction under Crucible, paving the way for post_session_signatures driver once consensus endpoints are unblocked. SIG-CR-001-OOB-DOS classification unchanged: honest-zero for fund-loss, informational DoS-only.

R6 closure: produced STRAT-S9-final-handoff-runbook-v6-51-10.md capturing the full 5-round (R1 multi-program, R2 cargo, R3 bankrun-N4, R4 PROP-EVM-010, R5 post_session_payload) outcome matrix. Five open signals all carry either a credible-harness proof or an honest-zero reproduction with env-block classifier. submit_ready unchanged at 1. next-session carry-forward queue (BR-MBOX-001, BR-LRBT-002, BR-CONS-002, BR-XR-LIGHT) queued for night-shift cron replay via hermes/scripts/anchor-test-each.sh.

BR-XR-LIGHT replay closed SIG-XR-001-ROLLBACK as validator-backed honest-zero. Temporary yarn shim allowed Anchor.toml test script to dispatch node_modules/.bin/ts-mocha without repo config changes. First run reached N4 but failed on local BigInt encoding; fixed amountLeBytes.writeBigUInt64LE(BigInt(wrongAmount.toString())) in gitignored Lombard source clone. Rerun: ccip.ts 11/11 passing in 40s; N4 specifically passed and proved failed executeOfframp rolls back mailbox messageInfo.status to Delivered (enum byte 1 at offset 8), not Handled (enum byte 2). No submission-ready Lombard finding.

BR-CL-001 (CLAdapter _lastPayload/_lastBurnedAmount cache redirect): NSS: CLAdapter cached payload redirects a later CCIP lock to an attacker payload added inside tests/Bridge.ts 'With Chainlink Adapter' describe. Sequence: signer1 seeds bridgeSource.deposit(CHAIN_ID, attackerReceiver, 50000000) so aCLAdapter._lastPayload/_lastBurnedAmount captures sha256(attacker payload) + 45000000 net. Then onRamp (router impersonated) calls aTokenPool.lockOrBurn({receiver: victimReceiver, amount: AMOUNT}). adapter.initiateDeposit hits the cached branch -> LockedOrBurned emits with amount=45000000 (not ccipAmount), destPoolData=sha256(seeded.payload) not sha256(victim payload), aCLAdapter pulls ccipAmount-seededNetAmount=55000000 from aTokenPool (stuck), and bTokenPool.releaseOrMint then mints 45000000 to attacker on chain B. Full set green: 21 passing (2s) across nss/PropEvmCrossLayerDivergence + Bridge. Strategy STRAT-S12 captures heuristic + carry-forward. SIG-CR-001-OOB killed by Rust regression (test_nss_noncanonical_offsets_rejected_as_leftover_data). Legacy Chainlink adapter proof is the green submission candidate for the recorded hunt, with caveat that LombardTokenPoolV2 has superseded it on production routes.

R2 honest-zero: PROP-XR-EVM-011B — partner-Bridge B>A impossible without source-side compromise. BridgeV2's `_withdraw` always mints the amount encoded in `decodeMsgBody(msgBody)`, which is set authoritatively by `mailbox.send` on the source chain at deposit time. Confirmed with new test 'BridgeV2._withdraw always mints amount from source-side msgBody — B>A cannot occur' inside nss/PropEvmCrossLayerDivergence.ts. Full suite green: 22 passing (2s). The new assertion closes PROP-XR-EVM-011's open side (v6.51.13): the pool returns 'destinationAmount=sourceDenominatedAmount=A' but the destination BridgeV2 mint amount is bound to source-side encoded amount A, not attacker-controlled B.

R3 honest-zero (engineering_blocker): Bascule partner-program off-rollback test (N5 in tests/ccip.ts) cannot be added in this session because the existing 'incoming CCIP bridge operation' harness uses the legacy LombardTokenPool (not LombardTokenPoolV2) and a baked bascule monitoring path. Adding a fresh program-load + bascule init inside the same PDA namespace as ccip.ts requires a new anchor test with the Bascule IDL/program not currently loaded into Workspace. Carry-forward: do R3 in a dedicated validator session that loads bascule/src via workspace key.

R4 engineering_blocker: adding three new Crucible actions (action_release_or_mint_via_bridge_gmp_receive, action_release_or_mint_with_bascule_denied, action_post_session_signatures_after_fail) requires compile-trial cycles and test re-runs in the 8s stateful mode (per v6.51.10 R5 4781/0-crash run habit). The current session exhausted its budget on the previous rounds; rather than risk regressing the proven 4781/0-crash run with an unfinished scaffold, we classify R4 as engineering_blocker and carry forward.

R5 mainnet reachability — v6.51.13 BR-CL-001 honest-zero on production. Probed legacy CLAdapter 0x2298d44CFDa0ceAfE7F5Ef5FD587B67d7b9b1Fbb via Alchemy for 6 major CCIP chain selectors (5009297550715157269 ETH, 11381663582735840572 BNB, 15971525465260171326 BASE, 4949039107704359120 ARB, 16281711391670634445 POL, 5224473277236331295 OP). All returned 0x000...000 from `getChain(uint64 selector)` view. Legacy Bridge contract 0xa869817b48b25eee986bdf4be04062e6fd2c418b `getAdapter(bytes32)` returned address(0). Conclusion: legacy CLAdapter is fully un-wired on production mainnet. Lombard Finance has migrated all chains to LombardTokenPoolV2 + BridgeV2, which does NOT surface the redirect exploit (R2 honest-zero confirms B>A can't happen without source-side compromise). v6.51.13 BR-CL-001 is therefore honest-zero on production. mainnet.json explicitly tags CLAdapter=deprecated for all 12+ supported chains. Evidence: evidence/evm-fork-mainnet-probe.log.
